title: zieria adenodonta
intro: Zieria adenodonta, also known as the small-leaved zieria or small-leaved currant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. Traditionally, the leaves and stems of Zieria adenodonta have been used by indigenous Australians for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Zieria adenodonta can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and has also been used in traditional Aboriginal medicine for its purported healing properties.
